│  ✅ test_case_58                                                             │
│  ├──   Input:              What does the Handbook Style Guide page say       │
│  │                         about GitLab's general communications             │
│  │                         practices are detailed at GitLab                  │
│  │                         Communication, but beyond?                        │
│  │     Actual Output:      The Handbook Style Guide states that GitLab's     │
│  │                         general communications practices are detailed     │
│  │                         at [GitLab                                        │
│  │                         Communication](/handbook/communication/), but     │
│  │                         beyond those, there is some channel-specific      │
│  │                         guidance available. It directs readers to the     │
│  │                         “list of resources below” (under *Related         │
│  │                         Resources*) for links to other guides.            │
│  │                         Handbook-specific style guidance is primarily     │
│  │                         covered by                                        │
│  │                         (https://handbook.gitlab.com/docs/markdown-gu…    │
│  │                         and                                               │
│  │                         (editing-handbook/_index.md#naming-pages-and-…    │
│  │                         In the absence of handbook-specific guidance,     │
│  │                         users should follow:                              │
│  │                         1. GitLab's [Writing Style                        │
│  │                         Guidelines](/handbook/communication/#writing-…    │
│  │                         or                                                │
│  │                         2. the [Documentation Style                       │
│  │                         Guide](https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/development…    │
│  │     Expected Output:    GitLab's general communications practices are     │
│  │                         detailed at GitLab Communication, but beyond      │
│  │                         those we do have some channel-specific            │
│  │                         guidance available. See the list of resources     │
│  │                         below for links to other guides.                  │

│                                                                              │
│  ❌ test_case_60                                                             │
│  ├──   Input:              What does the Handbook Style Guide page say       │
│  │                         about Communication - Markdown guide - GitLab     │
│  │                         Documentation guidelines - Documentation style    │
│  │                         Gui?                                              │
│  │     Actual Output:      The Handbook Style Guide page references these    │
│  │                         resources as part of its **Related                │
│  │                         Resources**, listing them as external or          │
│  │                         complementary style and documentation             │
│  │                         guidance. Specifically:                           │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         - The **[Markdown                                 │
│  │                         guide](https://handbook.gitlab.com/docs/markd…    │
│  │                         is cited as the primary source for                │
│  │                         handbook-specific formatting rules.               │
│  │                         - **[GitLab Documentation                         │
│  │                         guidelines](https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/develo…    │
│  │                         and the **[Documentation style                    │
│  │                         Guide](https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/development…    │
│  │                         are included in the list of related resources,    │
│  │                         indicating they serve as supplementary            │
│  │                         references—especially when handbook-specific      │
│  │                         guidance is absent.                               │
│  │                         - The Handbook Style Guide states that *“In       │
│  │                         the absence of handbook-specific guidance,        │
│  │                         follow: 1. GitLab's [Writing Style                │
│  │                         Guidelines](/handbook/communication/#writing-…    │
│  │                         or 1. the [Documentation Style                    │
│  │                         Guide](https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/development…    │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         Thus, these documentation-focused guides are      │
│  │                         treated as fallback authorities—not primary       │
│  │                         for handbook content, but authoritative where     │
│  │                         the handbook itself does not prescribe a rule.    │
│  │     Expected Output:    - GitLab Communication - Markdown guide -         │
│  │                         GitLab Documentation guidelines -                 │
│  │                         Documentation style Guide - GitLab style          │
│  │                         guides - Pajamas Design System - Marketing        │
│  │                         site content style guide - Blog style guide       │

│                                                                              │
│  ✅ test_case_62                                                             │
│  ├──   Input:              What does the Acquisitions Handbook page say      │
│  │                         about Below is a set of general, yet not          │
│  │                         strict, characteristics of companies that are     │
│  │                         a potenti?                                        │
│  │     Actual Output:      The Acquisitions Handbook states that below is    │
│  │                         a set of general, yet not strict,                 │
│  │                         characteristics of companies that are a           │
│  │                         potential fit for GitLab’s acquisition            │
│  │                         process. These include:                           │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         - Having built features or functionalities        │
│  │                         aligned with GitLab’s long-term strategy          │
│  │                         - Employing engineers who meet GitLab’s           │
│  │                         standard hiring guidelines                        │
│  │                         - Ideally offering a solution that accelerates    │
│  │                         GitLab’s maturity in a product category or        │
│  │                         fits into new categories on GitLab’s roadmap      │
│  │                         - Having an R&D team size under 30 people         │
│  │                         - Being willing to reimplement their products     │
│  │                         into GitLab using Ruby and Go                     │
│  │                         - Being willing to sunset old customers within    │
│  │                         90 days or less, with an option to transition     │
│  │                         them to GitLab                                    │
│  │     Expected Output:    Below is a set of general, yet not strict,        │
│  │                         characteristics of companies that are a           │
│  │                         potential fit for our acquisition process:        │

│  ❌ test_case_63                                                             │
│  ├──   Input:              What does the Acquisition Process page say        │
│  │                         about This is a detailed view of our              │
│  │                         acquisition process?                              │
│  │     Actual Output:      The Acquisition Process page states: “This is     │
│  │                         a detailed view of our acquisition process.”      │
│  │                         It further explains that the process comprises    │
│  │                         five key stages:                                  │
│  │                         1. Pipeline Building                              │
│  │                         2. Exploratory                                    │
│  │                         3. Early Diligence                                │
│  │                         4. Confirmatory Due Diligence                     │
│  │                         5. Integration                                    │
│  │     Expected Output:    This is a detailed view of our acquisition        │
│  │                         process. For more information about our           │
│  │                         acquisitions approach visit our acquisitions      │
│  │                         handbook.                                         │

│  ├──   Input:              What does the GitLab Alliances Handbook page      │
│  │                         say about commitment to helping customers and     │
│  │                         partners succeed - how we contribute our time,    │
│  │                         technol?                                          │
│  │     Actual Output:      The GitLab Alliances Handbook states:             │
│  │                         “lp enterprises lead the digital                  │
│  │                         transformation necessary to compete               │
│  │                         effectively in the market today. The              │
│  │                         commitment to helping customers and partners      │
│  │                         succeed — how we contribute our time,             │
│  │                         technology and know-how to improve the            │
│  │                         communities where we work and live and grow       │
│  │                         our business — is a journey that is               │
│  │                         transparent, ethical and honorable.”              │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         (Note: The phrase “lp enterprises” appears to     │
│  │                         be a formatting or OCR artifact in the source     │
│  │                         text and is not further clarified in the          │
│  │                         provided context.)                                │
│  │     Expected Output:    The commitment to helping customers and           │
│  │                         partners succeed - how we contribute our time,    │
│  │                         technology and know-how to improve the            │
│  │                         communities where we work and live and grow       │
│  │                         our business - is a journey that is               │
│  │                         transparent, ethical and honorable. We demand     │
│  │                         high performance and high standards from          │
│  │                         ourselves and our partners.                       │

│  │     Actual Output:      Once you have the above items, follow these       │
│  │                         steps to add yourself to the Partners page:       │
│  │                         1. Sign into gitlab.com and navigate to the       │
│  │                         home project of (https://gitlab.com), found       │
│  │                         (https://gitlab.com/gitlab-com/marketing/digi…    │
│  │                         Click on the **'Fork'** button at the top         │
│  │                         right to make a copy of the repository within     │
│  │                         your account.                                     │
│  │                         2. Next, click on the **'Web IDE'** button to     │
│  │                         make changes to specific YAML files.              │
│  │                         3. Navigate to the `/content` directory in the    │
│  │                         left pane and locate the `apps.yml` file          │
│  │                         within the folder. Click on the file to open      │
│  │                         it within the Web IDE.                            │
│  │     Expected Output:    Once you have the above items, follow these       │
│  │                         steps to add yourself to the Partners page:       │

│  ├──   Input:              What does the GitLab Integration Instructions     │
│  │                         page say about Choose the app's category          │
│  │                         accordingly?                                      │
│  │     Actual Output:      The GitLab Integration Instructions state:        │
│  │                         “Choose the app's category accordingly. The       │
│  │                         code block is to be added **to the end** of       │
│  │                         the category list it belongs to.” This means      │
│  │                         the YAML entry for your app must be placed at     │
│  │                         the end of the appropriate category section       │
│  │                         (e.g., under `category: Category`) in the         │
│  │                         `apps.yml` file.                                  │
│  │     Expected Output:    Choose the app's category accordingly. The        │
│  │                         code block is to be added to the end of the       │
│  │                         category list it belongs to.                      │

│  ✅ test_case_76                                                             │
│  ├──   Input:              What does the Board of Directors and Corporate    │
│  │                         Governance page say about is intended to          │
│  │                         capture GitLab Board practices, but we evolve     │
│  │                         these over time and the page?                     │
│  │     Actual Output:      The Board of Directors and Corporate              │
│  │                         Governance page states that it is intended to     │
│  │                         capture GitLab Board practices, but these         │
│  │                         practices evolve over time and the page may       │
│  │                         not reflect all changes. The source of truth      │
│  │                         for Board details is GitLab's Investor            │
│  │                         Relations site.                                   │
│  │     Expected Output:    This page is intended to capture GitLab Board     │
│  │                         practices, but we evolve these over time and      │
│  │                         the page may not capture all changes. The         │
│  │                         source of truth for Board details is GitLab's     │
│  │                         Investor Relations site.                          │

│       Status ┃ Metric             ┃ Score ┃ Threshold ┃ Reason               │

╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│                                                                              │
│  ❌ test_case_77                                                             │
│  ├──   Input:              What does the Board of Directors and Corporate    │
│  │                         Governance page say about the documented          │
│  │                         process?                                          │
│  │     Actual Output:      The Board of Directors and Corporate              │
│  │                         Governance page outlines a structured,            │
│  │                         time-bound documented process for board           │
│  │                         meetings, covering preparation, material          │
│  │                         handling, distribution, approval, and             │
│  │                         post-meeting follow-up. Key elements include:     │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         - **Material Submission & Review**: All           │
│  │                         materials must be submitted to the Chief Legal    │
│  │                         Officer (CLO) and CFO for review within 48        │
│  │                         hours of receipt. Once approved, the CFO          │
│  │                         authorizes the Corporate Paralegal to upload      │
│  │                         them into Nasdaq Boardvantage (NBV) and attach    │
│  │                         them to the corresponding NBV meeting.            │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         - **Deadline & Upload Requirements**: The         │
│  │                         deadline for document upload is 2 p.m. PT,        │
│  │                         seven days before the board meeting. At that      │
│  │                         time, all presentations and content must be       │
│  │                         finalized with no further changes.                │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         - **Sharing & Access Control**: The Staff         │
│  │                         Executive Business Associate (EBA) to the CFO     │
│  │                         manages sharing settings. Materials are shared    │
│  │                         with the E-Group, individual board members        │
│  │                         (not via the @board alias), Board Observers,      │
│  │                         and the outside legal firm. Any exceptions        │
│  │                         require explicit confirmation by the CLO and      │
│  │                         must be communicated to the Corporate             │
│  │                         Paralegal before distribution.                    │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         - **Agenda Linking & Notification**: The Staff    │
│  │                         EBA to the CFO links all materials in the         │
│  │                         Google Doc BOD Agenda for each session and        │
│  │                         ensures materials are always linked and shared    │
│  │                         appropriately. Seven days before the meeting,     │
│  │                         the CFO emails the Board notifying them that      │
│  │                         materials are ready for review in NBV,            │
│  │                         including highlights or action items.             │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         - **Minutes & Recordkeeping**: The CLO            │
│  │                         maintains the official Minutes of the meeting     │
│  │                         and submits them for Board approval. PDF          │
│  │                         versions of all materials—including               │
│  │                         presentations, exhibits, approved Minutes, and    │
│  │                         administrative documents—are securely stored      │
│  │                         in both NBV and a dated Google Drive folder.      │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         - **Post-Meeting Debrief**: The E-Group holds     │
│  │                         a debrief in the first E-Group meeting            │
│  │                         following the Board meeting.                      │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         - **Administrative Deck Responsibility**: The     │
│  │                         CFO is the Directly Responsible Individual        │
│  │                         (DRI) for the Administrative deck and shares      │
│  │                         it with the CLO, Corporate Paralegal, Staff       │
│  │                         EBA to the CFO, and Staff EBA to the CEO at       │
│  │                         least one week before the meeting.                │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         - **Agenda Content Standards**: Key materials     │
│  │                         presented include E-Group and CEO memos (up to    │
│  │                         four pages plus four exhibits per E-Group         │
│  │                         member), topic-specific memos (unlimited          │
│  │                         exhibits), Finance memos (unlimited exhibits),    │
│  │                         and the Annual Operating Plan (as memo or         │
│  │                         slide deck). The Administration Session           │
│  │                         includes Committee Chairperson readouts and       │
│  │                         highlights items requiring Board approval.        │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         This process emphasizes accountability,           │
│  │                         timeliness, secure access control, version        │
│  │                         finality, and cross-functional coordination.      │
│  │     Expected Output:    1. Current members of the Board of Directors      │
│  │                         1. Board of Directors Job Description             │

│  │                         Chairperson, Management DRI, and their Sr.        │

│                                                                              │
│  ❌ test_case_80                                                             │
│  ├──   Input:              What does the IT Enterprise Applications page     │
│  │                         say about the documented process?                 │
│  │     Actual Output:      The provided context does not state that the      │
│  │                         IT Enterprise Applications page itself            │
│  │                         documents any specific process. Instead, it       │
│  │                         describes the **role and scope** of the IT        │
│  │                         Enterprise Applications team:                     │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         > “The **IT Enterprise Applications** oversees    │
│  │                         the business technology architecture and          │
│  │                         systems that power GitLab as a business           │
│  │                         entity. We ensure that the systems are            │
│  │                         fit-for-purpose, available, reliable,             │
│  │                         integrated and secure. We continue to build       │
│  │                         business aligned technology to run operations,    │
│  │                         layering integration and automations to           │
│  │                         support remote work, enhance team member and      │
│  │                         customer experience, and generate value.”         │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         Process documentation (e.g., Quote to Cash        │
│  │                         workflows, Standard Operating Procedures, or      │
│  │                         How-to Guides) is referenced elsewhere — such     │
│  │                         as under *Procedures* (e.g., [Standard            │
│  │                         Operating                                         │
│  │                         Procedures](https://internal.gitlab.com/handb…    │
│  │                         and [How to                                       │
│  │                         Guides](https://internal.gitlab.com/handbook/…    │
│  │                         and *Quote to Cash Documentation* (including      │
│  │                         Zero Touch and Sales-Assisted flow diagrams).     │
│  │                         But these are separate sections, not              │
│  │                         statements made *on the IT Enterprise             │
│  │                         Applications page* about a documented process.    │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         Thus, the IT Enterprise Applications page — as    │
│  │                         reflected in the context — does **not describe    │
│  │                         or reference a specific documented process**;     │
│  │                         it outlines the team’s mission and                │
│  │                         responsibilities.                                 │
│  │     Expected Output:    2. Application EcoSystem We Support - Zuora       │
│  │                         (CPQ & Billing) - Zuora Revenue - Tesorio -       │
│  │                         NetSuite - Coupa - Stripe - Navan Travel -        │
│  │                         Navan Expense - Avalara - Adaptive Planning -     │
│  │                         Xactly - Mavenlink - DocuSign eSignature -        │
│  │                         ServiceNow - Tropic - Supplier IO - UI Path -     │
│  │                         Workato - Salesforce - Ownbackup                  │

│              │                    │       │           │ Applications but     │
│              │                    │       │           │ lacks the specific   │

│              │                    │       │           │ process. The         │
│              │                    │       │           │ fourth and fifth     │
│              │                    │       │           │ nodes discuss        │
│              │                    │       │           │ 'Quote to Cash       │
│              │                    │       │           │ Documentation' and   │
│              │                    │       │           │ process flow         │
│              │                    │       │           │ diagrams, but none   │
│              │                    │       │           │ provide the list     │
│              │                    │       │           │ of applications or   │
│              │                    │       │           │ the documented       │
│              │                    │       │           │ process requested.   │
│              │                    │       │           │ Since every node     │
│              │                    │       │           │ is irrelevant, the   │
│              │                    │       │           │ ranking of           │
│              │                    │       │           │ relevant nodes       │
│              │                    │       │           │ cannot be            │
│              │                    │       │           │ evaluated,           │
│              │                    │       │           │ resulting in the     │
│              │                    │       │           │ lowest score.        │
│        FAIL  │ Contextual Recall  │ 0.00  │ 0.50      │ The score is 0.00    │
│              │                    │       │           │ because the          │
│              │                    │       │           │ expected output      │
│              │                    │       │           │ lists specific       │
│              │                    │       │           │ applications         │
│              │                    │       │           │ (e.g., Zuora,        │
│              │                    │       │           │ NetSuite, Stripe),   │
│              │                    │       │           │ but none of these    │
│              │                    │       │           │ are mentioned in     │
│              │                    │       │           │ the retrieval        │

│  │                         work takes place. This is used as our             │

│  │                         capacity planning, and is helpful with roadmap    │

│  ├──   Input:              What does the Infrastructure Reference Links      │
│  │                         page say about Monthly Milestones - BT Infra      │
│  │                         2021-07 - BT Infra 2021-08 - BT Infra 2021-09     │

│  │     Actual Output:      The Infrastructure Reference Links page lists     │
│  │                         the following Monthly Milestones:                 │
│  │                         - BT Infra 2021-07                                │
│  │                         - BT Infra 2021-08                                │
│  │                         - BT Infra 2021-09                                │
│  │                         - BT Infra 2021-10                                │
│  │                         - BT Infra 2021-11                                │
│  │                         - BT Infra 2021-12                                │
│  │                         - BT Infra 2022-01                                │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         These are described as part of the team’s         │
│  │                         lightweight time tracking and capacity            │
│  │                         planning approach, used to map issues and         │
│  │                         epics to the time period in which most of the     │
│  │                         work occurs—supporting roadmap planning. The      │
│  │                         links for each milestone are provided, though     │
│  │                         the URL for BT Infra 2021-10 is cut off in the    │
│  │                         source text (ending with “/milestones/5”).        │

│  │                         Infra FY22-Q3 - BT Infra FY22-Q4 - Fiscal Year    │
│  │                         Milestones - BT Infra FY22-2H - BT Infra          │
│  │                         FY23-1H - BT Infra FY23-2H                        │

│                                                                              │
│  ✅ test_case_83                                                             │
│  ├──   Input:              What does the Infrastructure Reference Links      │
│  │                         page say about gitlab?                            │
│  │     Actual Output:      The Infrastructure Reference Links page           │
│  │                         provides information about GitLab’s internal      │
│  │                         infrastructure resources and labeling             │
│  │                         conventions, specifically for the Business        │
│  │                         Technology Engineering Infrastructure team. It    │
│  │                         includes:                                         │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         - A link to the public repository group           │
│  │                         hosting non-sensitive infrastructure code:        │
│  │                           (https://gitlab.com/gitlab-com/business-tec…    │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         - Guidance on standardized labels (e.g.,          │
│  │                         `bt-infra::wishlist`, `bt-infra-service::aws`,    │
│  │                         `bt-infra-service::sandbox-cloud`,                │
│  │                         `bt-infra-service::demo-systems`) used across     │
│  │                         issues, merge requests, and epics in the          │
│  │                         `gitlab.com/gitlab-com` namespace — but           │
│  │                         explicitly *not* in the `gitlab-org`              │
│  │                         namespace, as the team does not work on           │
│  │                         projects there.                                   │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         - Links to filtered views (issues, merge          │
│  │                         requests, epics) for each label, hosted on        │
│  │                         GitLab’s group-level issue boards and search      │
│  │                         interfaces.                                       │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         - Clarification that these labels support         │
│  │                         issue tracking and board organization for         │
│  │                         infrastructure-related work, including managed    │
│  │                         services like AWS, Sandbox Cloud, HackyStack,     │
│  │                         and Customer Success Demo Systems.                │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         No broader definition or description of GitLab    │
│  │                         the company or product is provided — the          │
│  │                         content is strictly operational and internal      │
│  │                         to GitLab’s infrastructure team practices.        │
│  │     Expected Output:    -                                                 │
│  │                         gitlab.com/gitlab-com/business-technology/eng…    │
│  │                         - The group with all repositories for Business    │
│  │                         Technology Engineering Infrastructure that        │
│  │                         don't contain sensitive information that          │
│  │                         should be hosted on ops.gitlab.net.               │
│  └── Metrics                                                                 │
│       Status ┃ Metric             ┃ Score ┃ Threshold ┃ Reason               │

│  ✅ test_case_84                                                             │
│  ├──   Input:              What does the Application Accessibility page      │
│  │                         say about GitLab CorpSec and Enterprise           │
│  │                         Applications teams are committed to ensuring      │

│  │     Actual Output:      The Application Accessibility page states that    │
│  │                         the GitLab CorpSec and Enterprise Applications    │
│  │                         teams are committed to ensuring accessibility     │
│  │                         across their applications for the widest          │
│  │                         possible audience, and they actively work to      │
│  │                         support all team members by enabling services     │
│  │                         and features already available within the         │
│  │                         products they use.                                │

│  │                         all team members by enabling services and         │
│  │                         features already available within the products    │
│  │                         we use.                                           │

│  │                         “Business Continuity Plan is the process          │
│  │                         involved in creating a system of prevention       │
│  │                         and recovery from potential threats to GitLab.    │
│  │                         The plan ensures that personnel and assets are    │
│  │                         protected and are able to function quickly in     │
│  │                         the event of a business disruption.”              │
│  │     Expected Output:    Business Continuity Plan is the process           │
│  │                         involved in creating a system of prevention       │
│  │                         and recovery from potential threats to GitLab.    │
│  │                         The plan ensures that personnel and assets are    │
│  │                         protected and are able to function quickly in     │
│  │                         the event of a business disruption.               │

│  ✅ test_case_88                                                             │
│  ├──   Input:              What does the Business Continuity Plan page       │
│  │                         say about Procedures can be found here in the     │
│  │                         event that an incident needs to be escalated      │
│  │                         within Gi?                                        │
│  │     Actual Output:      The Business Continuity Plan page states that     │
│  │                         procedures for escalating an incident within      │
│  │                         GitLab can be found in the SIRT Incident          │
│  │                         Response documentation, specifically under        │
│  │                         “Internal Engagement & Escalation for             │
│  │                         High-Severity Incidents.” Additionally, SIRT      │
│  │                         maintains an on-call schedule to ensure           │
│  │                         ongoing coverage and cross-training for           │
│  │                         incident response, and individual system          │
│  │                         outages should be escalated to the respective     │
│  │                         system owners per the Tech Stack.                 │
│  │     Expected Output:    Procedures can be found here in the event that    │
│  │                         an incident needs to be escalated within          │
│  │                         GitLab. SIRT maintains an on-call schedule to     │
│  │                         ensure ongoing coverage and cross-training for    │
│  │                         incident response procedures.                     │

│                                                                              │
│  ✅ test_case_89                                                             │
│  ├──   Input:              What does the Business Continuity Plan page       │
│  │                         say about Individual system outages should be     │
│  │                         escalated to system owners per the Tech Stack?    │
│  │     Actual Output:      Individual system outages should be escalated     │
│  │                         to system owners per the [Tech                    │
│  │                         Stack](https://gitlab.com/gitlab-com/www-gitl…    │
│  │                         as stated in the Business Continuity Plan.        │
│  │                         System owners are responsible for managing BCP    │
│  │                         for such outages, including maintaining plans     │
│  │                         to ensure key operational functions remain        │
│  │                         viable, understanding service level               │
│  │                         agreements, escalation paths, and backup          │
│  │                         operations when needed. These plans are           │
│  │                         reviewed and updated annually or after            │
│  │                         significant organizational changes.               │
│  │     Expected Output:    Individual system outages should be escalated     │

│  ✅ test_case_94                                                             │

│  │                         through additional procedures when handling       │

│  │                         states that the finance systems admin team        │
│  │                         goes through additional procedures when           │
│  │                         handling Access Requests—particularly because     │
│  │                         many of the provisioned finance systems (e.g.,    │
│  │                         Adaptive Insights, Avalara, Coupa, Navan,         │
│  │                         NetSuite, Stripe, Zuora Billing, Zuora            │
│  │                         Revenue) are SOX systems and subject to the       │
│  │                         SOX Program. These additional procedures          │
│  │                         include:                                          │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         - Ensuring or clarifying that the requested       │
│  │                         access is specific to the exact role or           │
│  │                         permission in the target system (e.g.,            │
│  │                         specifying “Custom Auditor (read only)” in        │
│  │                         NetSuite instead of a vague “read only access     │
│  │                         to NetSuite”).                                    │
│  │                         - Referring to a system-specific matrix for       │
│  │                         access request requirements.                      │
│  │                         - Seeking approval from the Business Owner for    │
│  │                         the exact role and/or permission being            │
│  │                         requested—based on who is listed as the owner     │
│  │                         in the Tech Stack—and aligning with baseline      │
│  │                         entitlements.                                     │
│  │                         - Provisioning the user in the system and         │
│  │                         adding them to the corresponding Okta or          │
│  │                         Google group if needed.                           │
│  │                         - Taking a timestamped screenshot of the user     │
│  │                         record post-provisioning, attaching it to the     │
│  │                         access request issue, and then closing the        │
│  │                         issue.                                            │
│  │                         - Assuming the request is for the Production      │
│  │                         environment unless explicitly stated otherwise    │
│  │                         in the Access Request.                            │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         These steps supplement the standard GitLab        │
│  │                         Access Request process.                           │
│  │     Expected Output:    The finance systems admin team goes through       │
│  │                         additional procedures when handling Access        │
│  │                         Requests.                                         │
│  └── Metrics                                                                 │
│       Status ┃ Metric             ┃ Score ┃ Threshold ┃ Reason               │
│      ━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━  │
│        PASS  │ Answer Relevancy   │ 0.78  │ 0.50      │ The score is 0.78    │
│              │                    │       │           │ because the actual   │
│              │                    │       │           │ output includes      │
│              │                    │       │           │ some relevant        │
│              │                    │       │           │ information about    │
│              │                    │       │           │ SOX compliance and   │
│              │                    │       │           │ additional           │
│              │                    │       │           │ procedures, but it   │
│              │                    │       │           │ also contains        │
│              │                    │       │           │ statements that      │
│              │                    │       │           │ provide background   │
│              │                    │       │           │ context or           │
│              │                    │       │           │ assumptions not      │
│              │                    │       │           │ directly             │
│              │                    │       │           │ addressing the       │
│              │                    │       │           │ specific             │
│              │                    │       │           │ additional           │
│              │                    │       │           │ procedures           │
│              │                    │       │           │ mentioned in the     │
│              │                    │       │           │ input, preventing    │
│              │                    │       │           │ a higher score.      │
│        PASS  │ Faithfulness       │ 1.00  │ 0.50      │ The score is 1.00    │
│              │                    │       │           │ because there are    │
│              │                    │       │           │ no contradictions    │
│              │                    │       │           │ between the actual   │
│              │                    │       │           │ output and the       │
│              │                    │       │           │ retrieval context,   │
│              │                    │       │           │ indicating perfect   │
│              │                    │       │           │ faithfulness.        │
│        PASS  │ Contextual         │ 1.00  │ 0.50      │ The score is 1.00    │
│              │ Precision          │       │           │ because all          │
│              │                    │       │           │ relevant nodes are   │
│              │                    │       │           │ ranked before any    │
│              │                    │       │           │ irrelevant nodes.    │
│              │                    │       │           │ The first three      │
│              │                    │       │           │ nodes directly       │
│              │                    │       │           │ support the input,   │
│              │                    │       │           │ with the first       │
│              │                    │       │           │ node explicitly      │
│              │                    │       │           │ stating 'The         │
│              │                    │       │           │ finance systems      │
│              │                    │       │           │ admin team goes      │
│              │                    │       │           │ through additional   │
│              │                    │       │           │ procedures when      │
│              │                    │       │           │ handling Access      │
│              │                    │       │           │ Requests.' The       │
│              │                    │       │           │ fourth and fifth     │
│              │                    │       │           │ nodes are            │
│              │                    │       │           │ irrelevant as they   │
│              │                    │       │           │ do not address the   │
│              │                    │       │           │ finance systems      │
│              │                    │       │           │ admin team's         │
│              │                    │       │           │ additional           │
│              │                    │       │           │ procedures, and      │
│              │                    │       │           │ they are correctly   │
│              │                    │       │           │ ranked lower.        │
│        PASS  │ Contextual Recall  │ 1.00  │ 0.50      │ The score is 1.00    │
│              │                    │       │           │ because the          │
│              │                    │       │           │ sentence in the      │
│              │                    │       │           │ expected output is   │
│              │                    │       │           │ directly present     │
│              │                    │       │           │ in the 1st node in   │
│              │                    │       │           │ retrieval context,   │
│              │                    │       │           │ perfectly            │
│              │                    │       │           │ supporting the       │
│              │                    │       │           │ expected output.     │

╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│                                                                              │
│  ✅ test_case_95                                                             │
│  ├──   Input:              What does the Finance Systems Access Requests     │
│  │                         page say about Adaptive Insights - Avalara -      │
│  │                         Coupa - Navan - NetSuite - Stripe - Zuora         │
│  │                         Billing - Zuora Re?                               │
│  │     Actual Output:      The Finance Systems Access Requests page          │
│  │                         states that Adaptive Insights, Avalara, Coupa,    │
│  │                         Navan, NetSuite, Stripe, Zuora Billing, and       │
│  │                         Zuora Revenue are all finance systems that are    │
│  │                         provisioned and considered SOX systems—thus       │
│  │                         subject to the [SOX                               │
│  │                         Program](https://internal.gitlab.com/handbook…    │
│  │                         As such, the finance systems admin team           │
│  │                         follows additional procedures when handling       │
│  │                         access requests for these systems.                │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         Additionally, each system has specific access     │
│  │                         request (AR) requirements:                        │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         - **Adaptive Insights**: The AR must              │
│  │                         explicitly list the **Permissions Set (one)**     │
│  │                         being requested. James Shen (Sr. Director of      │
│  │                         FP&A) must approve every single Adaptive          │
│  │                         Insights access request. Access is structured     │
│  │                         by “Levels” aligned with departments, and most    │
│  │                         users should not have access to all levels due    │
│  │                         to confidentiality.                               │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         - **Avalara**: While listed as a SOX system,      │
│  │                         no system-specific AR requirements are            │
│  │                         detailed in the provided context.                 │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         - **Coupa**: Requestors/buyers are always         │
│  │                         assigned the ***Custom User*** role; elevated     │
│  │                         access requires Procurement approval.             │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         - **Navan**: The AR must explicitly state the     │
│  │                         **exact role** being requested **and** which      │
│  │                         policies—though most requests received are for    │
│  │                         Navan Admins, and team members are typically      │
│  │                         auto-provisioned.                                 │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         - **NetSuite**: The AR must explicitly state      │
│  │                         the **exact NetSuite role** being requested       │
│  │                         before Business Owner approval.                   │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         - **Stripe**: The AR must explicitly state the    │
│  │                         **exact role** being requested **and** which      │
│  │                         **Stripe Accounts**, as roles are not             │
│  │                         customizable.                                     │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         - **Zuora Billing**: The AR must explicitly       │
│  │                         list the role being requested **for each          │
│  │                         module**: Platform, Billing, Payments,            │
│  │                         Finance, Commerce, and Reporting.                 │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         - **Zuora Revenue**: The AR must explicitly       │
│  │                         state the **exact ZREV role(s)** being            │
│  │                         requested before Business Owner approval.         │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         No further details are provided for Avalara in    │
│  │                         the given context.                                │
│  │     Expected Output:    - Adaptive Insights - Avalara - Coupa - Navan     │
│  │                         - NetSuite - Stripe - Zuora Billing - Zuora       │
│  │                         Revenue                                           │
│  └── Metrics                                                                 │
│       Status ┃ Metric             ┃ Score ┃ Threshold ┃ Reason               │
│      ━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━  │
│        PASS  │ Answer Relevancy   │ 1.00  │ 0.50      │ The score is 1.00    │
│              │                    │       │           │ because the actual   │
│              │                    │       │           │ output contains no   │
│              │                    │       │           │ irrelevant           │
│              │                    │       │           │ statements and       │
│              │                    │       │           │ fully addresses      │
│              │                    │       │           │ the input query.     │
│        PASS  │ Faithfulness       │ 0.85  │ 0.50      │ The score is 0.85    │
│              │                    │       │           │ because the actual   │
│              │                    │       │           │ output contains      │
│              │                    │       │           │ minor                │
│              │                    │       │           │ contradictions: it   │
│              │                    │       │           │ incorrectly          │
│              │                    │       │           │ implies that the     │
│              │                    │       │           │ context does not     │
│              │                    │       │           │ provide              │
│              │                    │       │           │ system-specific AR   │
│              │                    │       │           │ requirements for     │
│              │                    │       │           │ Avalara, while the   │
│              │                    │       │           │ context clearly      │
│              │                    │       │           │ states that the AR   │
│              │                    │       │           │ must explicitly      │
│              │                    │       │           │ state the exact      │
│              │                    │       │           │ role being           │
│              │                    │       │           │ requested, roles     │
│              │                    │       │           │ are not              │
│              │                    │       │           │ customizable, and    │
│              │                    │       │           │ lists the            │
│              │                    │       │           │ available roles.     │
│              │                    │       │           │ However, these       │
│              │                    │       │           │ contradictions are   │
│              │                    │       │           │ limited and do not   │
│              │                    │       │           │ severely undermine   │
│              │                    │       │           │ overall              │
│              │                    │       │           │ faithfulness.        │
│        PASS  │ Contextual         │ 1.00  │ 0.50      │ The score is 1.00    │
│              │ Precision          │       │           │ because the only     │
│              │                    │       │           │ relevant node is     │
│              │                    │       │           │ ranked first, and    │
│              │                    │       │           │ all irrelevant       │
│              │                    │       │           │ nodes are ranked     │
│              │                    │       │           │ after it, which is   │
│              │                    │       │           │ exactly the ideal    │
│              │                    │       │           │ ordering for         │
│              │                    │       │           │ contextual           │
│              │                    │       │           │ precision.           │
│        PASS  │ Contextual Recall  │ 1.00  │ 0.50      │ The score is 1.00    │
│              │                    │       │           │ because every item   │
│              │                    │       │           │ in the expected      │
│              │                    │       │           │ output is fully      │
│              │                    │       │           │ supported by the     │
│              │                    │       │           │ 1st node in the      │
│              │                    │       │           │ retrieval context,   │
│              │                    │       │           │ which contains the   │
│              │                    │       │           │ exact same list.     │
│                                                                              │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

│                                                                              │
│  ✅ test_case_96                                                             │
│  ├──   Input:              What does the Quote to Cash Documentation page    │
│  │                         say about This spreadsheet provides a mapping     │
│  │                         of fields from the data models of Zuora           │
│  │                         Billing, Custo?                                   │
│  │     Actual Output:      The Quote to Cash Documentation page states:      │
│  │                         *“This spreadsheet provides a mapping of          │
│  │                         fields from the data models of Zuora Billing,     │
│  │                         CustomersDot and GitLab. This document            │
│  │                         highlights some of the overlap and                │
│  │                         dependencies between the systems currently.”*     │
│  │                                                                           │
│  │                         The sentence explicitly names **Zuora             │
│  │                         Billing**, **CustomersDot**, and **GitLab** —     │
│  │                         not “Custo” alone — as the three systems whose    │
│  │                         field mappings are included. “Custo” appears      │
│  │                         to be a truncation of “CustomersDot”, which is    │
│  │                         the full and correct system name referenced in    │
│  │                         the documentation.                                │
│  │     Expected Output:    This spreadsheet provides a mapping of fields     │
│  │                         from the data models of Zuora Billing,            │
│  │                         CustomersDot and GitLab. This document            │
│  │                         highlights some of the overlap and                │
│  │                         dependencies between the systems currently.       │

│              │                    │       │           │ no contradictions    │

│              │                    │       │           │ output and the       │
│              │                    │       │           │ retrieval context,   │

│              │                    │       │           │ faithfulness.        │

│              │                    │       │           │ contexts are         │

│              │                    │       │           │ contain the exact    │
│              │                    │       │           │ path                 │
│              │                    │       │           │ 'enterprise-apps -   │
│              │                    │       │           │ business-technolo…   │
│              │                    │       │           │ -                    │
│              │                    │       │           │ bt-finance-operat…   │
│              │                    │       │           │ -                    │
│              │                    │       │           │ financesystemshelp   │
│              │                    │       │           │ -                    │
│              │                    │       │           │ bt-integrations'.    │
│              │                    │       │           │ For example, the     │
│              │                    │       │           │ first node lacks     │
│              │                    │       │           │ the full path, the   │
│              │                    │       │           │ second node does     │
│              │                    │       │           │ not explicitly       │
│              │                    │       │           │ list the requested   │
│              │                    │       │           │ path, the third      │
│              │                    │       │           │ node omits the       │
│              │                    │       │           │ exact path, the      │
│              │                    │       │           │ fourth node is       │

│  │                         that the license never becomes a barrier to       │
│  │                         adoption for the client.                          │
